model._comment: Schema source, named once. 'env' points into the environments registry by name (live OSSYS read, espace-safe) — the canonical environment the whole estate derives from. 'path' is the extracted-file fallback. Use 'ossys' instead of 'env' only for a standalone connection with no registry.

overrides._comment: Naming + structural directives, keyed to THIS sample's entities (Sales / Billing / ServiceCenter) — swap the refs for your own. Every section is optional; omit one to take the faithful default. A rename 'from' is EITHER logical { module, entity } OR physical { schema, table } (never both). 'migrationDependencies.path' names a separate operator-curated row inventory (see examples/migration-dependencies.sample.json) that feeds the MigrationData lane; those kinds are excluded from the bootstrap complement so the lanes stay disjoint.

emission._comment: WHAT the publish bundle emits, and HOW the data lanes write. ssdt = the schema; staticSeeds / migrationDependencies / bootstrap = the three data lanes (each defaults true; set one false to suppress that lane). The lanes are disjoint by construction: bootstrap covers the complement of (static ∪ migration), so no row loads twice. 'bootstrapAllData':true flips bootstrap to cover EVERY data-bearing kind — the full first-deploy snapshot (V1's AllEntitiesIncludingStatic) — and skips the static+migration lanes (default false). 'dataVerification':'validateBeforeApply' prepends a symmetric-EXCEPT drift guard (THROW 50000 on drift) ahead of every data MERGE; 'standard' (default) emits the MERGEs alone (byte-identical, CDC-silent on idempotent redeploy). 'deleteScope' adds a convergent 'WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE … DELETE' arm (a tenant/partition gate) to the static+migration MERGEs — omit it for the upsert-only default; bootstrap is additive-only and ignores it. 'sqlproj':true also drops an SDK-style Microsoft.Build.Sql project (ProjectionCatalog.sqlproj + Script.PostDeployment.sql, whose post-deploy :r-includes the static+migration lanes; bootstrap stays a separate post-publish step) so the publish bundle is a buildable SSDT project — 'dotnet build' → .dacpac (default false).
emission._dataStaging_comment: HOW large data kinds write (static, migration, AND bootstrap lanes). A single inline 'MERGE … USING (VALUES …)' hits SQL Server error 8623 (the optimizer's plan-complexity wall) at ~25-30k rows, so above 'threshold' rows a kind stages its rows through a #temp table and runs ONE 'MERGE … USING #temp' (and, for cyclic kinds, a single set-based Phase-2 'UPDATE … FROM #fk') inside one atomic XACT_ABORT batch — no VALUES ceiling, all-or-nothing. 'mode':'auto' (default) stages above 'threshold' (default 1000); 'inline' NEVER stages (the locked-down / managed-OutSystems escape hatch — accepts the ~30k ceiling where even baseline #temp + BEGIN TRAN rights are unwanted); 'tempTable' ALWAYS stages. 'indexThreshold' (default 100000) is the row count above which a staged #temp also gets a CLUSTERED INDEX on its PK (built after the bulk insert, dropped with the #temp) so the MERGE merge-joins instead of hash-joining — measured ~33-37% faster at 100k/250k/500k; the default gates at the proven-win floor. The staged form needs only the baseline rights the identity-seed path already uses, so 'auto' is portable wherever the current seed path runs.

policy._comment: OPT-IN overlays. Omit this block for faithful vanilla emission. 'tightening.interventions' are TRACKED operator decisions (kinds: foreignKey / uniqueIndex / categoricalUniqueness); each carries a kind + id. 'transformGroups' BOTH run by default — a missing group is enabled — so list one with enabled:false only to suppress it.

profiler._comment: Source-data evidence. 'live' profiles the source DB (connection from PROJECTION_MSSQL_CONN_STR, never the config — D9) for the readiness data-dealbreaker checks and evidence-based tightening; 'fixture' (the default) carries none.

readiness._comment: The agreed shape defaults to model.env (cloud-dev) — name 'schema' here only to override it. 'confirm' lists the environments checked against that shape.

synthetic._comment: How `from: synthetic` generates data (THE_SYNTHETIC_DATA_DESIGN §11). Hybrid by cardinality: a column with ≤ preserveCardinalityMax distinct values keeps its real distribution; above that it is synthesized. 'preserve'/'synthesize' override per logical column NAME. --seed/--scale are the per-run overrides; the per-flow 'correction' carries the richer per-column PII→Faker intent (generate a first draft with `projection propose-correction`).
